text: The Etihad erupts as Kevin De Bruyne unleashes a thunderbolt from twenty yards out that leaves the goalkeeper absolutely stranded!
Whatever Mikel Arteta said at halftime has worked, because Bukayo Saka dances through the defense and slots it home with ease.
It is pandemonium in the away end as Mohamed Salah capitalizes on the defensive error to tap in what is surely the winner for Liverpool!
Unbelievable scenes here! Son Heung-min curls a magnificent effort into the top corner, silencing the home crowd completely.
Erling Haaland rises highest to meet the cross, powering a header past the despairing dive of the keeper to double the lead for Man City.
The celebrations are cut short immediately as the assistant referee raises his flag against Marcus Rashford for straying beyond the last man.
Whistle blown! A clumsy challenge by Harry Maguire brings down the attacker just outside the box, giving away a dangerous free-kick.
VAR has intervened and disallowed the goal, spotting a handball by Gabriel Jesus in the build-up much to the frustration of the Arsenal fans.
Bruno Fernandes is furious, screaming at the referee, but the whistle had clearly gone for a push before the ball hit the net.
Possession is turned over as Rodri is penalized for a tactical foul to stop the counter-attack near the center circle.
The referee reaches into his pocket and flashes a yellow card at Casemiro for that cynical lunge on the winger.
It goes from bad to worse for Chelsea as Reece James is shown a straight red card for violent conduct!
Granit Xhaka argues his case, but his name is going into the book for persistent infringement throughout the second half.
Cristian Romero flies into the tackle with studs up, leaving the official no choice but to dismiss him from the field of play.
A tactical yellow card taken by Luke Shaw there, pulling the shirt to prevent a certain goal-scoring opportunity.
Jurgen Klopp looks to his bench and decides to bring on Darwin Nunez to replace the tiring Cody Gakpo in search of a goal.
A standing ovation at Old Trafford as Kobbie Mainoo makes way for the experienced Christian Eriksen.
Pep Guardiola is not happy with the control, so he swaps Jack Grealish for the more direct Phil Foden on the left flank.
It's a double change for Spurs; James Maddison and Richarlison are coming off to be replaced by Bissouma and Werner.
The captain Martin Odegaard hands over the armband as he jogs off, with Emile Smith Rowe taking his place in midfield.
Play has stopped and the physios are rushing on; Kevin De Bruyne is clutching his hamstring and it does not look good.
Silence falls over the stadium as Lisandro Martinez is stretchered off the pitch with what appears to be a serious ankle injury.
Thiago Silva signals to the bench immediately after that clearance, indicating he cannot continue.
A nasty collision of heads between Virgil van Dijk and the striker has forced the referee to halt play for a concussion check.
Neymar is writhing in pain on the turf, holding his right knee, and the medical staff are signaling for a substitution.
Manchester United are content to just knock the ball around the back four, trying to draw the opposition out of their shell.
What a wasted opportunity! Alexander Isak breaks free but sends his shot high and wide into the stands.
Declan Rice intercepts the pass and looks to drive forward, but the passing lane is blocked by a wall of defenders.
The tempo has really dropped here at Anfield, with both sides looking exhausted as we approach the final minutes.
Trent Alexander-Arnold whips a dangerous cross into the box, but it evades everyone and rolls harmlessly out for a goal kick.
Bedlam at the Emirates! Gabriel Martinelli strikes at the death to steal all three points for Arsenal!
Pick that one out! James Maddison curls a beauty from distance that leaves the keeper clutching at thin air.
Beautiful one-touch football from City ends with Phil Foden sweeping the ball into the bottom corner.
Lightning fast on the break! Mohamed Salah sprints clear and coolly finishes past the onrushing goalie.
A towering header from Virgil van Dijk crashes off the underside of the bar and crosses the line!
The flag is up on the far side, denying Kai Havertz what would have been a debut goal for Chelsea.
Crunching tackle by Rodri in the midfield, and the referee blows immediately for a free kick.
Kieran Trippier clips the heels of the winger to stop the break, a clear professional foul.
Handball given against William Saliba! He blocked the shot with an outstretched arm right on the edge of the area.
Aston Villa hold their line perfectly, catching Nunez offside for the third time today.
Disaster for William Saliba! He trips the last man and the referee produces the red card immediately.
Jordan Henderson talks himself into the book, arguing too aggressively about that last decision.
He's off! Yves Bissouma receives a second yellow card for diving and Spurs are down to ten men.
The crowd jeers as Andre Onana is booked for taking far too long over the goal kick.
A reckless challenge from Romeo Lavia earns him a yellow, though he's lucky it wasn't a different color.
Unai Emery shuffles his pack, withdrawing Leon Bailey for the defensive stability of Youri Tielemans.
Fresh legs for Newcastle as Callum Wilson and Miguel Almiron enter the fray replacing Isak and Gordon.
Raheem Sterling departs to a chorus of boos from his former fans, replaced by Mykhailo Mudryk.
Erik ten Hag brings on Harry Maguire to shore up the defense for these final tense minutes.
Chasing the game, West Ham introduce Mohammed Kudus hoping for a spark of creativity in the final third.
Reece James pulls up chasing that ball and signals to the bench; looks like another hamstring issue.
Concern here for Alisson Becker who landed awkwardly after claiming that high cross.
The stretcher is called onto the pitch at Goodison Park for Andre Gomes after that horrific tackle.
Play is halted as Fabian Schar needs treatment for a cut above his eye.
Jurrien Timber goes down clutching his knee without any contact, which is never a good sign for the Arsenal defender.
How has he missed that? Erling Haaland blasts the ball over the bar from six yards out!
What a save! Emi Martinez stretches to his left to fingertip the ball around the post.
Brighton continue to dominate possession, moving the ball patiently from side to side looking for an opening.
James Ward-Prowse delivers a curling corner but it's headed clear by the first defender.
So close! Alejandro Garnacho rattles the woodwork with a fierce drive that had the keeper beaten.
He's done it again! Ollie Watkins runs onto the through ball and blasts it into the roof of the net to give Villa the lead!
Stunning! Just stunning! Trent Alexander-Arnold whips a free-kick over the wall and into the top corner, leaving the keeper rooted.
The corner is swung in, and Gabriel Magalhaes rises above the crowd to power a header downward and across the line!
Against the run of play, Crystal Palace have snatched a goal! Eze dances through the midfield and finds the bottom corner.
Bernardo Silva creates something out of nothing, chipping the goalkeeper from a tight angle to silence the Old Trafford crowd.
The celebrations are premature as the linesman's flag goes up against Nicolas Jackson for leaning just offside.
It's a penalty! The referee points to the spot after Enzo Fernandez trips the attacker inside the area.
Play is pulled back for a handball by Diogo Dalot, who seemingly controlled the cross with his upper arm.
A roar of disapproval from the home fans as the referee waves away claims for a foul on Bukayo Saka.
The goal will not stand! VAR review confirms that Haaland pushed the defender in the back before heading it in.
Referee Michael Oliver has seen enough of the time-wasting and flashes a yellow card at the Brentford goalkeeper.
Shocking challenge! Destiny Udogie goes in two-footed and is rightfully shown a straight red card.
Tempers are flaring now, and both Joelinton and Rice are booked for that scuffle in the center circle.
Diogo Jota receives a second yellow card for a cynical trip, and Liverpool are down to nine men!
A tactical foul by Kalvin Phillips stops the counter, and he accepts the inevitable yellow card without complaint.
A huge roar welcomes Kevin De Bruyne back to the pitch as he replaces Julian Alvarez for the final twenty minutes.
Sean Dyche makes a defensive switch, bringing on Tarkowski to replace the striker Calvert-Lewin to hold onto this lead.
Brighton are forced into a change as the injured Mitoma makes way for Simon Adingra.
Ange Postecoglou rolls the dice, throwing on Timo Werner for the final assault on the opposition goal.
The youngster Rico Lewis trots off after a fantastic performance, high-fiving Kyle Walker who takes his spot.
Worrying scenes as Pedro Neto pulls up clutching his hamstring in full flight; his race is run today.
Play is stopped immediately! The captain Harry Kane has taken a heavy blow to the head and needs assessment.
Ederson is down and looks in considerable pain after that brave collision with the striker.
The physios are signaling that Raphael Varane cannot continue, likely a recurrence of his groin problem.
Ben Chilwell is limping heavily on the touchline and shakes his head; he won't be able to carry on.
Off the post! Cole Palmer beats the keeper with a clever dink but sees it rebound off the upright!
Incredible reflexes from Guglielmo Vicario to tip that point-blank header over the bar!
Wolves are struggling to get out of their own half, with passing becoming sloppy under pressure.
Martin Dubravka comes out confidently to catch the corner kick and relieve the pressure on his defense.
The referee blows the whistle for half-time, bringing an end to a pulsating 45 minutes of football at Stamford Bridge.
Can you believe it?! Declan Rice rises deepest in stoppage time to glance a header past the keeper and win it for Arsenal!
Simply magnificent! Alexis Mac Allister thunders a volley into the top corner from fully thirty yards out!
A clinical counter-attack from Manchester United is finished off by Marcus Rashford who slots it beneath the diving goalkeeper.
Alexander Isak twists, turns, leaves two defenders on the floor, and curls a masterpiece inside the far post!
The deadlock is broken at Goodison Park as Abdoulaye Doucoure smashes home the rebound following a chaotic scramble.
The assistant referee waits until the move is complete before raising his flag; Darwin Nunez had strayed just beyond the last defender.
Penalty shout waved away! Sterling goes down under contact from Walker, but the referee indicates he should get up.
Conor Gallagher is penalized for a push in the back on the edge of the box, presenting a dangerous set-piece opportunity.
After a lengthy check at the monitor, the referee overturns the goal decision due to a handball by Joelinton in the build-up.
The whistle goes for a high boot, as Sadio Mane caught the defender in the face while attempting an overhead kick.
A moment of madness from Raul Jimenez who flies into the goalkeeper and is shown a straight red card!
Yellow card for Bruno Guimaraes, who cynically pulled the shirt of the breaking attacker to stop the transition.
Diogo Dalot is sent off for dissent! Two yellow cards in the space of ten seconds for arguing with the official.
Anthony Gordon goes into the book for simulation; the referee was right there and saw no contact.
A harsh booking for Yves Bissouma, who seemed to win the ball cleanly before collecting the man.
Desperate times call for desperate measures as Eddie Howe makes a triple substitution to try and salvage a point.
A Premier League debut for the youngster Lewis Miley, who replaces the injured Sean Longstaff in the engine room.
More defensive reinforcement for City as Nathan Ake comes on for Phil Foden to lock down the left side.
Thomas Frank responds immediately to the goal by sending on Ivan Toney to add more physical presence up front.
Applause ripples around the stadium as Son Heung-min is withdrawn for Richarlison after a match-winning display.
The game stops as Nick Pope signals to the bench, clutching his shoulder after that awkward diving save.
You hate to see this; Wesley Fofana is in tears as he is stretchered off with what looks like a severe knee injury.
Play is paused for a head injury assessment on Harry Maguire following a clash of heads in the penalty area.
Kaoru Mitoma has pulled up mid-sprint and drops to the turf holding the back of his thigh.
Medical staff rush onto the field to attend to John Stones who took a heavy stud to the ankle.
David Raya produces a world-class save to claw the ball out of the top corner!
It hits the side netting! For a second, half the stadium thought that was in, but Gabriel Jesus missed by inches.
Luton Town are defending deep with all eleven men behind the ball, frustrating the opposition.
Bruno Fernandes tries a spectacular lob from the halfway line, but it drifts harmlessly wide.
The referee's whistle brings a chaotic first half to a close here at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium.
Absolute screaming pile-driver from Rodri! The net almost bursts off its hinges as City take the lead!
A chaotic scramble in the six-yard box ends with Nicolas Jackson poking the ball across the line for Chelsea.
It's a hat-trick for Erling Haaland! He coolly slots the penalty past the helpless goalkeeper to make it 3-0.
Bournemouth take a shock lead at Old Trafford! Dominic Solanke rises highest to head home the corner.
Wonderful vision from Martin Odegaard to find Trossard, who rifles it into the roof of the net from a tight angle.
The goal is chalked off! Richarlison was standing in an offside position when the shot was hit, obstructing the keeper's view.
Penalty to Chelsea! Raheem Sterling is brought down by a clumsy challenge from the defender inside the box.
Foul given on the edge of the area as James Maddison trips the advancing midfielder to halt the attack.
The flag stays down initially, but VAR eventually confirms that Marcus Rashford was leaning offside by a shoulder's width.
Handball! The referee points for a free kick after the ball strikes the arm of Virgil van Dijk just outside the D.
Red card! The referee dismisses Curtis Jones for serious foul play after a VAR review showed studs making contact with the ankle.
Booking for dissent. Bruno Fernandes just won't stop complaining to the official and finally talks himself into trouble.
A tactical yellow for Rodri, taking one for the team to stop a dangerous counter-attack near the halfway line.
Ashley Young is shown a second yellow and he's off! Everton are down to ten men with twenty minutes to go.
Ugly scenes here as Cristian Romero is booked for sparking a mass confrontation after a late tackle.
Mikel Arteta turns to his bench, bringing on Jorginho to control the midfield tempo for the closing stages.
A tactical tweak from Liverpool: Trent Alexander-Arnold makes way for Joe Gomez to add more defensive solidity.
Standing ovation for the goalscorer as Alexander Isak leaves the field, replaced by Callum Wilson.
Manchester City make a double change: Doku and Kovacic replace Grealish and Nunes to inject fresh energy.
Substitution for Spurs: The injured James Maddison is replaced by Hojbjerg in a forced change.
Play stops as Bukayo Saka stays down holding his ankle after that heavy tackle; this is a worry for Arsenal.
Serious concern for Kevin De Bruyne who signals to the bench immediately; it looks like a recurrence of his hamstring issue.
The physio is on to treat Raphael Varane, who seems to have pulled a muscle in that sprint for the ball.
Lengthy stoppage here as the goalkeeper Nick Pope receives treatment for a dislocated shoulder.
Lisandro Martinez is in agony on the turf, and the stretcher is being called for after an awkward landing.
Alisson Becker makes a comfortable save, gathering the weak shot into his chest without spilling a rebound.
End to end stuff! Gabriel Martinelli hits the post and on the rebound, Gabriel Jesus fires wide!
Man City are just keeping the ball now, frustrating the opposition with endless passing triangles in the midfield.
Huge chance wasted! Darwin Nunez is through on goal but scuffs his shot completely wide of the far post.
The referee blows for full time, ending a rather dull affair here at Villa Park with the score level.
Unstoppable! Jarrod Bowen cuts inside from the right and curls a delightful finish into the far corner to stun the visitors!
What a moment for the teenager! Kobbie Mainoo weaves through three defenders and slots it home for his first senior goal!
The corner is met by the head of Gabriel Magalhaes, who powers it past the keeper to restore Arsenal's lead!
And there it is! Mohamed Salah converts the penalty with ice-cold precision, sending the goalkeeper the wrong way.
Brighton are level! Kaoru Mitoma arrives at the back post to tap in the cross after a flowing team move.
The referee has blown his whistle; it seems Kyle Walker impeded the attacker's run into the box.
Hold on, VAR is checking for a potential offside against Nicolas Jackson in the buildup to that chance.
Free kick to Manchester United in a dangerous position after Bruno Fernandes was tripped right on the edge of the D.
The goal is disallowed! The ball had clearly gone out of play for a goal kick before the cross was delivered.
Cynical foul by Fabinho to break up the counter-attack, and he knows exactly what he's done there.
Antony is booked for simulation after diving in the penalty area under minimal contact.
Reece James sees red! A second yellow card for pulling back the winger and he's off for an early shower.
The referee loses patience with the time-wasting and flashes a yellow card at goalkeeper Emiliano Martinez.
A reckless challenge from Cristian Romero sees him go into the book, and he will now miss the next match through suspension.
Tempers boil over and Casemiro is shown a straight red card for grabbing an opponent by the throat!
Jurgen Klopp makes a triple substitution, bringing on Diaz, Gakpo, and Elliott to chase the game.
An emotional moment as Thiago Silva is substituted for the final time at Stamford Bridge, receiving a standing ovation.
Tactical change for Brentford: Maupay comes on for Wissa to add a different dimension to the attack.
Pep Guardiola withdraws Erling Haaland early, presumably to rest him for the Champions League clash mid-week.
Aston Villa replace the tiring John McGinn with Youri Tielemans to shore up the midfield.
Worrying signs for Spurs as James Maddison goes down holding his ankle without anyone near him.
Play is stopped for a concussion check after Harry Maguire took a heavy blow to the head.
Ben Chilwell signals to the bench immediately; it looks like another hamstring injury for the full-back.
The stretcher is out for Joelinton who seems to have landed awkwardly on his knee.
Kevin De Bruyne is limping heavily and it doesn't look like he'll be able to continue.
What a save! Alisson Becker somehow claws the header away from the top corner!
Darwin Nunez smashes the ball against the crossbar from five yards out! How did he miss that?
Manchester City are just recycling possession now, making the opposition chase shadows.
Bruno Fernandes attempts a speculative effort from distance but it sails high and wide into the stands.
A quiet period of play here at the Emirates as both teams look to settle into a rhythm.
It's in! The corner from Ward-Prowse causes chaos, and Kurt Zouma bundles it over the line!
What a counter-attack! Son Heung-min races clear and squares it for Brennan Johnson to tap into an empty net.
Absolute magic from Phil Foden! He receives the ball in the pocket, turns, and fires a rocket into the top corner.
The Villa Park crowd goes wild as Leon Bailey cuts inside and curls a beauty past the outstretched keeper!
Redemption for Darwin Nunez! After missing three chances, he finally finds the net with a powerful header.
No goal! The referee has spotted a foul on the goalkeeper by Calvert-Lewin in the crowded six-yard box.
The linesman's flag is up immediately; Ivan Toney mistimed his run and was clearly offside.
Whistle blows for a handball against Saliba, who misjudged the bounce of the ball completely.
A clear trip by Caicedo halts the progress of the midfielder, and the referee awards a free kick.
VAR check complete: The goal is disallowed because Martinelli was marginally offside in the build-up.
Lewis Dunk is shown a straight red card for dissent after shouting aggressively in the referee's face.
A tactical foul earns Jorginho a yellow card as he pulls back the breaking player near the center circle.
Reckless from Udogie! He flies into the tackle with studs showing and is lucky to escape with only a yellow.
Rodri goes into the book for kicking the ball away to delay the restart.
Second yellow card for Mario Lemina! He didn't need to make that challenge, and now Wolves are down to ten.
Double change for Arsenal: Gabriel Jesus and Zinchenko are coming on to replace Nketiah and Kiwior.
A huge cheer goes up as Kevin De Bruyne begins his warm-up, preparing to enter the fray.
Roberto De Zerbi makes a tactical switch, withdrawing the winger Adingra for the extra midfielder Milner.
It's the end of the night for Rasmus Hojlund, who worked hard but couldn't find a goal; Martial takes his place.
Pochettino rolls the dice, bringing on Mudryk to use his pace against the tired legs of the opposition defense.
Play is stopped as Ederson stays down after a heavy collision with the post.
Concern for Bukayo Saka who is limping heavily and signaling to the bench that he has a problem.
The game is paused while the medical team attends to a head injury for Harry Maguire.
It looks like a groin strain for hamstring for James Maddison; he's pulled up sharply and is shaking his head.
Lisandro Martinez is being stretchered off to applause from both sets of fans; it looks serious.
What a save! Vicario dives full length to his right to palm away the curling effort!
Off the bar! Alexander Isak dinks the keeper but sees his effort rebound off the woodwork.
Possession is dominated by Manchester City, but they are struggling to break down this low block.
Bruno Fernandes tries a through ball for Rashford, but it's overhit and rolls through to the keeper.
The referee checks his watch as we enter the final minute of stoppage time at Anfield.
Unbelievable drama! Dejan Kulusevski rises at the back post in the 98th minute to head home the winner for Spurs!
The net bulges! Anthony Gordon finishes off a rapid counter-attack, sliding the ball under the advancing goalkeeper.
Simply majestic! Kevin De Bruyne bends the free-kick over the wall and into the very top corner, leaving the keeper stranded.
Everton lead! Dwight McNeil smashes a half-volley from the edge of the box that flies through a sea of bodies into the net.
A mistake at the back allows Diogo Jota to pounce, and he makes no mistake, rounding the keeper to tap into the empty goal.
No penalty! VAR checks the challenge by William Saliba but decides he got a toe on the ball before the man.
The goal is ruled out for offside! Rasmus Hojlund thought he had scored, but he was inches ahead of the last defender.
Joao Palhinha commits a professional foul to stop the break, hauling down the attacker near the center circle.
The referee blows his whistle for a push in the box; Harry Maguire was all over the defender during that corner.
Handball given against Cristian Romero! His arm was in an unnatural position when the shot was fired.
Jordan Pickford receives a yellow card for taking an age with the goal kick, much to the annoyance of the home crowd.
It's a straight red! Malo Gusto catches the opponent high on the ankle with his studs, and VAR advises a dismissal.
Bruno Fernandes is booked for dissent, waving his arms in frustration at the linesman's decision.
A tactical booking for Kieran Trippier, who cynically trips the winger to prevent a 3-on-2 situation.
Conor Gallagher picks up a second yellow card for a late challenge and Chelsea are reduced to ten men!
Erik ten Hag looks to change the dynamic, bringing on Scott McTominay to replace the ineffective Amrabat.
Applause rings out as Phil Foden makes way for Jack Grealish after a masterclass in midfield creativity.
Mikel Arteta responds by sending on Leandro Trossard and Jorginho in a double change to regain control.
Brighton introduce the young striker Evan Ferguson, who replaces Danny Welbeck for the final ten minutes.
A defensive reinforcement for West Ham as Kurt Zouma comes on to help protect this slender lead.
Luke Shaw pulls up sharply holding his hamstring, and the bench is already preparing a replacement.
A sickening clash of heads between Raul Jimenez and the defender sees play stopped immediately for urgent medical attention.
Sven Botman is down in the box, clutching his knee in obvious distress as his teammates wave for the physio.
The game is paused as Ederson receives treatment for a cut on his leg following that courageous save.
Silence at the Emirates as Gabriel Jesus lands awkwardly and signals that he cannot continue.
What a fingertip save! David Raya flies across his goal to divert the curling shot wide of the post.
Ollie Watkins thinks he's scored, but his shot rattles the inside of the post and bounces clear!
Manchester City continue to probe the edge of the box, passing patiently and waiting for an opening.
Marcus Rashford finds space on the wing but his cross is too heavy and sails out for a goal kick.
The fourth official indicates there will be a minimum of five minutes of added time.
Absolute precision! Dominik Szoboszlai unleashes a rocket from 25 yards that nearly tears the net off the goal frame!
Fluid football from Arsenal results in Bukayo Saka tapping in at the back post to finish a wonderful team move.
A towering leap from Harry Maguire! He connects perfectly with the corner to power a header past the despairing keeper.
Pandemonium in the away end! Darwin Nunez has surely won it for Liverpool with a dramatic stoppage-time strike!
Alexander Isak weaves through the entire defense, dances around the keeper, and rolls the ball into the empty net. Majestic!
The flag goes up immediately, cutting the celebrations short for Son Heung-min who was clearly yards offside.
Penalty given! William Saliba trips the striker inside the box and the referee points to the spot without hesitation.
Handball! The referee blows the whistle after the ball strikes the outstretched arm of Virgil van Dijk.
Rodri pulls the shirt of the opponent to stop the counter-attack, committing a clear professional foul.
VAR intervenes and rules out the goal for a push by Gabriel Jesus on the defender just before the header.
He's off! Reece James drags down the last man and is shown a straight red card for denying a goal-scoring opportunity.
Bruno Fernandes talks himself into the book again, arguing aggressively with the referee about a throw-in decision.
Andre Onana receives a yellow card for taking far too long with the goal kick, testing the referee's patience.
It's a second yellow for Yves Bissouma! He dives in the penalty area and is sent for an early shower.
A nasty challenge from Cristian Romero sees him go into the referee's notebook; he was nowhere near the ball.
Jurgen Klopp looks to the bench and introduces Cody Gakpo for Luis Diaz to refresh the attack.
Erik ten Hag brings on Raphael Varane for Alejandro Garnacho to hold onto this slender lead.
A standing ovation at the Etihad as Kevin De Bruyne makes way for Mateo Kovacic.
Double substitution for Chelsea: Mudryk and Palmer come on to replace Sterling and Gallagher.
Forced change for Spurs as the injured James Maddison is replaced by Oliver Skipp.
Pedro Neto pulls up clutching his hamstring mid-sprint; that looks like a bad one for the winger.
Play is stopped for a head injury assessment after Harry Maguire clashed heads with the striker.
The stretcher is called for Lisandro Martinez who is writhing in clear distress on the turf.
Ederson is down and receiving treatment after colliding with the post while making that save.
Jurrien Timber goes down holding his knee without any contact, a very worrying sign for Arsenal.
Erling Haaland is through on goal but drags his shot wide of the post! Unbelievable miss!
What a save! Guglielmo Vicario flies to his right to tip the powerful header over the bar.
Man City are content to keep the ball in their own half, inviting the press from the opposition.
Off the post! Darwin Nunez smashes a volley against the upright and it bounces clear to safety.
The referee blows the whistle for half-time at Old Trafford with the score still deadlocked at 0-0.
History made at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium! Harry Kane breaks the scoring record with a precise finish into the bottom corner!
Out of nowhere! Diogo Dalot cuts onto his left foot and curls a sensational effort past the keeper to silence the home fans.
The counter-attack is lethal! Cole Palmer slides the ball through to Nicolas Jackson, who makes no mistake from close range.
It's a debut goal to remember! Sandro Tonali volleys home the cross to give Newcastle the lead within five minutes!
Pure persistence from Ollie Watkins! He fights off two defenders, stays on his feet, and pokes the ball into the net.
Disallowed! The referee has spotted a foul in the build-up where Ben White pushed the defender to create space.
The flag is raised late against Mohamed Salah, who had just strayed beyond the last defender before receiving the pass.
Penalty kick awarded! Casemiro slides in recklessly and takes out the man, leaving the referee with no other option.
Play is halted for a handball by Lucas Paqueta, who tried to control the high ball with his arm.
A tactical foul from Bruno Guimaraes stops the breakaway, resulting in a free kick near the halfway line.
Straight red card! Cristian Romero goes over the top of the ball with studs showing, a dangerous and reckless challenge.
A second yellow for Diogo Dalot means he's sent off! Manchester United will have to finish this game with ten men.
Booking for Kai Havertz, who kicked the ball away in frustration after the whistle had already gone.
The referee brandishes a yellow card to Jordan Ayew for persistent fouling throughout the second half.
Yves Bissouma is cautioned for diving in an attempt to win a penalty; the referee was perfectly placed.
Pep Guardiola makes a double substitution to close out the game: Stones and Ake replace Walker and Gvardiol.
A standing ovation for Bukayo Saka as he leaves the field, having run the defense ragged all afternoon.
Jurgen Klopp brings on Harvey Elliott to inject some energy into a tiring Liverpool midfield.
Substitution for Chelsea: Thiago Silva makes way for Disasi as Pochettino looks to manage the veteran's minutes.
Ange Postecoglou is forced into a change with Van de Ven limping off; Emerson Royal comes on in his place.
Awful scenes as Nick Pope writhes in pain holding his shoulder; the physios are calling for a stretcher immediately.
Play is stopped for a concussion protocol check after Raul Jimenez took a heavy blow to the head.
Reece James pulls up chasing a long ball and signals to the bench; the captain looks devastated.
Kevin De Bruyne is down and receiving treatment on his ankle following that late tackle.
Concern for Sven Botman who landed awkwardly on his knee and is unable to put any weight on it.
How did he miss?! Erling Haaland is unmarked six yards out but heads the ball wide of the post!
Incredible save! Alisson Becker somehow spreads himself to block the shot from point-blank range.
Arsenal are controlling the tempo now, moving the ball from side to side to tire out the opposition.
Bruno Fernandes attempts a long-range drive but it deflects off a defender and goes out for a corner.
It hits the woodwork! Darwin Nunez smashes a volley against the crossbar and it bounces away to safety.
The deadlock is broken! Gabriel Jesus receives the ball with his back to goal, spins the defender, and rifles it into the bottom corner.
Stamford Bridge rises to its feet as Cole Palmer coolly slots the penalty past the dive of the keeper.
A moment of individual brilliance! Eberechi Eze dribbles past three challenges and curls a majestic shot into the top bin.
Manchester United are back in it! Garnacho meets the cross with a spectacular overhead kick that leaves the stadium stunned.
It's a gift for Liverpool! The goalkeeper spills a simple catch and Darwin Nunez is there to tap into the empty net.
That is astonishing! Kevin De Bruyne hits a thunderbolt from 30 yards that crashes in off the underside of the bar.
Against the run of play, Brentford take the lead! Ivan Toney powers a header home from the corner kick.
The referee's whistle goes immediately; Bukayo Saka handled the ball before setting up the shot.
Flag up on the near side! Alexander Isak mistimed his run by a fraction of a second.
It's a penalty! Ruben Dias slides in clumsily and wipes out the attacker, leaving the referee no choice.
VAR Review: The goal is chalked off as Richarlison was blocking the goalkeeper's line of sight from an offside position.
Free kick to Arsenal on the edge of the box after Martin Odegaard was tripped by the retreating midfielder.
